abstract: The powerful effects of electronic word of mouth on employer branding and prehire outcomes suggest a need for employers to formulate effective responses to employer reviews on social media. Using machine learning and text-mining techniques, we identified three distinct types of employer responses to negative reviews (i.e., excuses, apologies, prosocial behavior) and two other types of responses to positive reviews (i.e., ingratiation, exemplification) from a Glassdoor data set. Integrating research on organizational impression management and stereotype content, we developed and tested a theoretical model of response types and their effects on talent attraction across two vignette experiments with undergraduate (Study 1) and working adult job seekers (Study 2). Across both studies, not responding to negative reviews resulted in the worst outcomes for employers. Results demonstrate that the effectiveness of responses differed by the target population; prosocial behavior was most effective among job-seeking professionals, whereas excuse and apology were more effective among students. While exemplification had positive effects in the student sample, neither assertive tactic had a significant effect on hypothesized outcomes in sample of job-seeking professionals. Furthermore, warmth and sincerity, but not competence, mediated the effect of responses on key prehire outcomes of employer reputation, organizational attraction, and job pursuit intentions. Taken as a whole, our study suggests that employer reviews represent both a threat and an opportunity. (PsycInfo Database Record (c) 2025 APA, all rights reserved).

ob.ai_reason: 使用机器学习文本挖掘但核心是印象管理招聘
ob.tldr: 基于印象管理理论，利用机器学习与文本挖掘技术识别雇主对Glassdoor评论的五类回复策略：针对负面评论的借口、道歉与亲社会行为，以及针对正面评论的讨好与典范行为。通过两组实验（学生与在职求职者）发现：不回复负面评论后果最差；亲社会行为对职场人士最有效，借口与道歉对学生更有效；仅温暖与真诚感（而非能力感）中介回复效果，影响雇主声誉、组织吸引力与求职意愿。

title: Managing online employer reviews: An impression management perspective for talent recruitment.
title_zh: 管理在线雇主评价：人才招聘的印象管理视角
